name: ST MARGARETS HOSPITAL DURHAM BH1
units: metres below ground level; typed AEG site-investigation record — depth = base of interval, cross-checked against Reduced Level column

intervals.verbatim: MADE GROUND (Loose, dark grey, fine sand with some fine to medium sized fragments of brick.)
MADE GROUND (Loose, pale brown, fine to medium sand with occasional fine to coarse brick fragments, ash and coal.)
MADE GROUND (Medium dense, dark grey, slightly clayey sand with some fine to coarse brick, ash and occasional clinker fragments.)
Loose, brown, slightly clayey, silty, fine SAND. (as sheet 2: Medium dense, brown, slightly clayey silty fine SAND, thinly bedded with sand sized coal fragments and clayey bands.)
annotations: below 4.50m BGL ... very thinly bedded with coarse sand sized coal fragments and clayey bands
below 9.00m BGL ... becoming less clayey with occasional fine gravel sized coal fragments
below 9.50m BGL ... becoming structured and possibly cross bedded

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense to dense, brown, slightly clayey, fine sandy SILT, very thinly interbedded with silty, micaceous (?), fine SAND.
annotations: at 15.00m BGL ... thinly bedded with fine sand sized coal fragments

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense, grey brown, slightly clayey (becoming very clayey with depth), silty fine SAND, thinly bedded with slightly clayey beds (coarse sand sized coal fragments).
Stiff, grey brown, silty, sandy CLAY with some fine to coarse gravel and coarse sand pockets.
Dense to very dense, brown, sandy, fine to coarse, subangular to subrounded GRAVEL with thin bands of stiff, brown grey, sandy, fine to coarse gravelly CLAY with some cobbles and coal fragments.
Very stiff, brown, sandy, gravelly CLAY with occasional cobbles. Gravels and cobbles composed of fine to coarse, subangular to rounded, sandstone, coal, shale and limestone.
Grey, fine grained, highly to completely weathered, thinly bedded MUDSTONE, friable and very weak.
annotations: rockhead ~28.40m; rotary drilling continued below (Sheet 5, Duplicate drillhole log)

intervals.verbatim: Grey, thinly bedded, highly weathered, sandy MUDSTONE, friable, very weak, with thin beds of muddy, fine grained, highly weathered, micaceous SANDSTONE, moderately weak.
annotations: thin rotary bed: description-to-depth registration is interpretive

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey, highly weathered, coaly SHALE, weak.
annotations: 29.15 - 29.20m ... thin band of broken lustrous COAL (unnamed, ~50mm)
thin rotary bed: description-to-depth registration is interpretive

intervals.verbatim: Grey, thinly bedded, moderately weathered, sandy MUDSTONE, friable, micaceous and weak.
annotations: thin rotary bed: description-to-depth registration is interpretive

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey, thinly to medium bedded, cross bedded, fresh to slightly weathered, muddy, fine to medium grained, micaceous SANDSTONE, moderately weak.
annotations: between 30.95 - 31.05m ... iron stained bands interbedded with thin beds of grey sandy friable, moderately weathered MUDSTONE, weak

intervals.verbatim: Grey, thinly bedded, sandy MUDSTONE, friable and weak.
Grey, fine grained, thinly bedded to medium bedded, micaceous, fresh SANDSTONE, cross bedded and moderately strong.
annotations: between 33.60 - 33.80m ... iron stained bands interbedded with thin beds of grey sandy friable, moderately weathered MUDSTONE, weak

notes: Modern typed AEG (Allied Exploration & Geotechnics Ltd) borehole record, borehole BH-01, 'St Margaret's Hospital, Crossgate, Durham NE', client Ove Arup & Partners (Newcastle), contract 1351, logged by G. Giles, status FINAL 15.7.92. Cable percussion (Pilcon Wayfarer 1500) to 28.90m then rotary (air flush) to 35.00m. Ground Level 65.53 m AOD; handwritten grid NZ 26880 42418 on sheet 1 matches header 426880,542418. Seven page images read: p1-p4 = cable-percussion strata sheets 1-4 of 4 (0-28.90m); p5 = a 'Duplicate' rotary DRILLHOLE LOG sheet 1 of 1 (28.90-35.00m, run details TCR/SCR/RQD + fracture index recorded but not strata); p6 = Site Location 1967 map (Figure 1, scale 1:10560, no strata, covers NZ24SE/315-317); p7 = Site Plan (scale 1:500, no strata, shows BH01/BH02/BH03 + trial pits, covers NZ24SE/315-317). All Thickness / Depth / Reduced-Level arithmetic cross-checks clean across every boundary; no clerk arithmetic slips found. COAL present only as a thin unnamed band 29.15-29.20m ('thin band of broken lustrous COAL') within a coaly SHALE bed; no seam name is given in the original and no pencil seam-correlation marginalia (LM/HTN/etc.) are present, so seam_marks is empty. Handwritten marks are reference/registration stamps ('NZ24SE/315', BGS registration) only. Intervals 12-14 are thin (0.2-0.3m) rotary beds: their depths are legibly printed (boundaries 29.10/29.30/29.60m, confirmed by RL 36.43/36.23/35.93) but assigning each stacked 'Main' description to its band is interpretive, hence confidence 'probable'. Water: cable-percussion water depths logged 16.90-24.60m over 01-03.06.92 (dry/moist above); rotary standing water 25.52m on 04.06.92, dry at completion. Methane monitored throughout drilling, none detected.
